findings.message: The planned gate does not enforce the stated outcome of "all routes in both light and dark mode." TASK-02 only checks `Business`, `Audit-Date`, `Status`, `Routes-Tested > 0`, and `S1-Blockers = 0`; it never validates `Modes-Tested` includes both `light,dark` or that route coverage is complete. A single-route, single-mode sweep could pass while violating the operator contract.

TASK-05 creates a second gate implementation path that the real startup-loop advance flow does not consume. `.claude/skills/startup-loop/modules/cmd-advance.md` loads `.claude/skills/startup-loop/modules/cmd-advance/s9b-gates.md`, and there is no runtime consumer of a proposed `scripts/src/startup-loop/s9b-gates.ts`. That means tests can pass against dead code while the live Markdown gate used by operators diverges.

The validation contract is not executable as written. `scripts/package.json` has a `test` script, but no `typecheck` or `lint` script, so `pnpm --filter scripts typecheck` and `pnpm --filter scripts lint` here and again at line 520 will fail before validating anything. The plan needs the actual repo-local validation path.

The plan is internally inconsistent on test scope. TASK-05 is introduced as "covering 7 gate scenarios," but the acceptance list immediately defines 8 cases, and the decision log later says the count changed from 7 to 8. The same mismatch appears earlier in the summary/task table/engineering coverage, which makes scope and confidence tracking unreliable.

Using `Audit-Date:` from frontmatter instead of deriving staleness from the directory slug is well grounded in the live sweep artifact schema and is a cleaner contract than the filename-parse pattern used by `GATE-LAUNCH-SEC`.
